DISTRICT ATTORNEY 
PUBLIC ADMINISTRATOR -
PUBLIC GUARDIAN 
JAMES D. BOITANO 
District Attorney 
HALL OF JUSTICE. 1125 THIRD STREET. P. O. BOX 148 
NAPA, CALIFORNIA 94558 
October 16, 1980 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
State of California, P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
RE: 
Napa County Conflict of Interest Code 
Dear Ladies and Gentlemen: 
707-253-4211 
As you know, Napa County has enacted a Conflict of Interest Code. 
However, over my strong objection, they have included the District 
Attorney as one of the individuals that must file fOrITl 730-State-
ment of Economic Interest for designated employees. 
Again, as you know, I am mandated by State law to file an almost 
exact duplicate of such a statement and my question to you is 
whether this is not an improper duplication of effort. 
In other words, since State law has preempted this particular area, 
is County legislation not improper? 
As a practical matter, this is another example of the bureaucratic 
duplication which is so costly in both time and money. 
JDB:clb 
cc: 
Mr. Albert J. Haberger 
County Administrator 
Mr. Stephen Hackett 
County Counsel 
ry truly yours, 
'- J. (){ =-
BOITANO

sections.question: Since State law has mandated that the District Attorney file an almost exact duplicate statement (Form 730-Statement of Economic Interest) and Napa County has included the District Attorney as one of the individuals that must file the same form under their Conflict of Interest Code, is this not an improper duplication of effort? Since State law has preempted this particular area, is County legislation not improper?
sections.question_synthetic: The District Attorney of Napa County questions whether requiring him to file duplicate Economic Interest statements under both state law and the county's Conflict of Interest Code constitutes improper duplication, given that state law has preempted this regulatory area.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a District Attorney to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document quality is poor with significant OCR/scanning artifacts in the header section, but the main body is legible. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are present, so question and conclusion text were synthesized from the letter's content. No FPPC response is included in this document.

Since State law has preempted this particular area, is County legislation not improper?

embedding.summary: District Attorney James D. Boitano writes to the FPPC to challenge Napa County's Conflict of Interest Code requirement that he file Form 730 under county law, arguing this duplicates the state-mandated filing requirement and represents improper county legislation that conflicts with state preemption.
